title: Mildly Sour Brazilian Lamb Stew
description: A dense and flavorful Brazilian stew made with lamb shanks and score fat, perfect for a family dinner. This dish is kid-friendly and kosher-friendly.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 300 degrees Fahrenheit.
Season the lamb shanks with salt and black pepper.
Heat the score fat in a large Dutch oven over medium-high heat.
Sear the lamb shanks until browned on all sides, then remove and set aside.
Add the onion and garlic to the pot and cook until the onion is translucent.
Add the lime juice, tomato paste, and beef broth to the pot, stirring to combine.
Return the lamb shanks to the pot and bring the mixture to a boil.
Cover the pot and transfer it to the preheated oven.
Braise the stew for 2 1/2 hours, or until the lamb is tender.
Add the potatoes and carrots to the pot and continue to braise for an additional 30 minutes.
Remove the pot from the oven and let the stew cool slightly before serving.
